name: Spinach, Walnut and Raisin Boreks
recipeIngredient: 2 packages (10-oz each) frozen Chopped Spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, cook the onions in 2 tablespoons margarine until soft, not brown.
2. Add the well-squeezed spinach, egg, nuts, raisins, parsley, salt, pepper, and dill. Mix well. Taste for adequate seasoning.
3. Thaw the phyllo dough in the refrigerator for at least a day before using. Unwrap and unroll the dough, covering it with a damp, well-wrung towel to keep it from drying out as you work.
4. With scissors, cut the dough into half-length strips all at once. Work off one stack of these strips.
5. Brush some melted margarine along half the top strip of dough. Fold the ungreased half over the greased half and place a spoonful of filling in one corner. Fold the strip over, making a triangle.
6. Continue making triangles by folding the strip up like folding a flag, tucking under any ragged edges, and brushing the top of each triangle with margarine. Sprinkle with poppy seeds.
7. Place as many triangles as possible on one baking tray. Repeat filling strips of dough until all filling is used.
8. Bake the boreks, one tray at a time, in the upper third of a preheated 375°F oven for about 20-25 minutes or until golden. Turn the pan once after about 15 minutes.
9. Cool the boreks on a wire rack for a few minutes before serving to prevent condensation from forming and softening the crust.
10. Serve warm. Assembled boreks can be refrigerated for up to a day or two before baking, or after baking frozen on a cookie sheet and then boxed. Rewarm boreks in a 375°F oven for about 10 minutes.
